questions.question_text: What is the primary purpose of an offer in contract law?
options: To express willingness to enter into a contract
To reject a previous offer
To negotiate terms of a contract
To terminate an existing contract

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements is true about acceptance in contract law?
options: Acceptance must be communicated to the offeror
Acceptance can be implied without any communication
Acceptance can be revoked after being communicated
Acceptance must always be in writing

questions.question_text: What happens if an offer is met with a counter-offer?
options: The original offer remains open
The original offer is rejected
The original offer is accepted
The original offer is automatically accepted

questions.question_text: In contract law, what is the 'mirror image rule'?
options: Acceptance must match the offer exactly
Offers must be identical to be valid
Contracts must be written in mirrored language
Offers and acceptances must be made simultaneously

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a valid form of acceptance?
options: Silence
Verbal agreement
Written agreement
Conduct indicating acceptance
